summary: CI workflow fix that stops forked repositories from deriving Docker package image names from the fork owner/name. The workflows now set a global IMAGE_REPO to doldecomp/melee and construct images as $IMAGE_REPO/$IMAGE_SUFFIX, removing per-event push/pull_request repository selection and lowercasing shell logic. dump-asm and publish-pages also changed their deploy/push guards from explicit master/main refs to the repository default branch.
what_changed: Added a top-level workflow env variable IMAGE_REPO: doldecomp/melee to build-melee.yml, dump-asm.yml, and publish-pages.yml.

Replaced event-dependent image repository selection using EVENT_NAME, PUSH_REPO_NAME, PR_REPO_NAME, case/esac, and tr lowercasing with direct IMAGE construction: echo "IMAGE=$IMAGE_REPO/$IMAGE_SUFFIX" >> $GITHUB_ENV.
Applied the simplified image-name construction to build-linux, build-windows, check-issues, dump-asm, and gen-pages image suffixes.
Removed shell: bash from dump-asm and publish-pages image-name steps where it was only needed for the previous shell script complexity.
Changed dump-asm push guard from github.ref == 'refs/heads/master' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' to github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch.
Changed publish-pages deploy guard from github.ref == 'refs/heads/master' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' to github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch.
smart_moves: Centralized the package repository as a workflow-level IMAGE_REPO instead of recomputing it in every job step.
Removed fragile GitHub event branching between push and pull_request image sources, which was causing forks to try pulling nonexistent fork-owned images.
Kept per-job IMAGE_SUFFIX values, so each job still selects the correct image tag while sharing one authoritative repository name.
Replaced hardcoded master/main branch checks with the repository default_branch expression, making deploy/push behavior less branch-name-specific.
Used a small stopgap CI simplification after discussion confirmed the fork-image behavior was an active pain point.
decomp_lessons: Infrastructure changes can directly affect decomp velocity: broken fork CI can spam contributors with failed runs and discourage long-lived forks from pushing work.
For shared project workflows, defaulting to upstream-maintained Docker packages may be less surprising than requiring every fork to have a fresh matching package image.
When workflow logic is intended to support forks, document or encode the default behavior explicitly rather than relying on derived repository names.
Prefer repository-default-branch checks over explicit master/main checks in reusable workflows.
naming_conventions: Introduced IMAGE_REPO as the shared workflow-level variable for the Docker image repository.
Preserved IMAGE_SUFFIX for job-specific image tags such as build-linux:latest, build-windows:latest, check-issues:latest, dump-asm:latest, and gen-pages:latest.
Used GitHub Actions context names github.ref_name and github.event.repository.default_branch for default-branch gating.
review_feedback: r-burns opened the PR because pushing to a fork caused actions to pull an image based on the fork owner/name, which did not exist.
r-burns noted in the body that if hardcoding was the correct solution, the case/esac and tr logic should also be removed; the final diff does remove that logic.
ribbanya explained that the previous intended workflow was for forks to pull their own packages, but acknowledged the setup was poorly documented.
ribbanya suggested an alternative variable-based opt-in/opt-out approach for using doldecomp packages versus local fork packages, but that is not what the final diff implements.
Discussion recognized the package-per-repository approach was intended to support non-vanilla/custom forks, but long-lived vanilla forks found the behavior unintuitive.
ribbanya described this as something to revisit after dtk and later agreed to implement a stopgap fix when the pain point recurred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that revisit CI package image selection after dtk migration discussion.
Search for workflow variables controlling whether forks use doldecomp packages or fork-local packages.
Check whether project documentation was updated to explain Docker package expectations for forked repositories.
Look for subsequent changes to GitHub Actions image names or package publishing behavior in .github/workflows.
